name: Great Lakes Credit Union
description_short: Great Lakes Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving the Chicago area since 1938, offering checking, savings, loans, and investment products with a community-focused approach.
description_long:
Great Lakes Credit Union was founded in 1938 and has operated for over 85 years as a member-owned financial cooperative. The organization positions itself around the philosophy of "banking for a greater good," emphasizing personalized service and community commitment. As a credit union, it is regulated by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) and provides NCUA Share Insurance on deposits.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial services across five main categories: banking products (checking, savings, money market accounts, certificates, and IRAs), lending solutions (personal loans, vehicle loans, home equity, credit cards, student loans, and mortgages through a partner), investment services (Easy Vest, certificates, IRAs, and financial advisory), insurance products (auto, homeowners, life, and accidental death insurance), and business banking (small business lending, cash management, and payment processing). The Chicago Uptown branch specifically provides in-person services including loan applications, account opening, ATM access, notary services, and medallion signature guarantees.

Great Lakes differentiates itself through its credit union structure, which emphasizes member benefits over profit maximization, and its integration of educational services including housing and education counseling. The organization serves both individual consumers and small business owners. The Chicago Uptown location offers extended Saturday hours (9am-1pm) and comprehensive branch services, positioning it as a full-service financial center rather than a limited-service branch.

As a credit union, Great Lakes is subject to membership eligibility requirements not clearly detailed on this webpage. While the site emphasizes personal service and community values, specific interest rate competitiveness, fee structures, and membership requirements would need to be verified separately. The reliance on a mortgage partner (Mortgage Forward) suggests some lending services are not handled in-house. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: NCUA-insured deposits provide federal protection up to applicable limits
Comprehensive service suite including banking, lending, investments, insurance, and business services all under one organization
Extended Saturday hours (9am-1pm) at Chicago Uptown location for working professionals
On-site notary and medallion signature guarantee services at branch
Instant-issue debit cards available for immediate use
Credit union structure theoretically prioritizes member benefits over profits
Free financial counseling services including housing and education counseling
cons: Membership eligibility requirements not transparently disclosed on public website, creating potential barriers to access
Mortgage services outsourced to third-party provider (Mortgage Forward) rather than handled in-house
Limited information on competitive interest rates and fee schedules available from this branch page
No indication of whether all products/services are available at every branch location
Website does not clearly specify minimum deposit or balance requirements for accounts
best_for: Chicago-area residents seeking full-service banking with a community-focused institution
Small business owners needing integrated business banking and lending services
Consumers prioritizing personal relationships and financial counseling over large institutional banking
Members seeking NCUA-insured deposit protection with investment and insurance options
diagnosis: Great Lakes Credit Union is best for Chicago-area residents and small business owners seeking comprehensive, relationship-based banking from a member-owned institution with community commitment. Primary caveat: membership eligibility requirements are not transparent on their public-facing website and should be verified before applying; consumers shopping rate-sensitive products should compare their specific rates against competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
meta_description: Member-owned credit union serving Chicago since 1938 offering checking, savings, loans, and investment products.
